ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Petition Pursuant To Probate Code 850
Off Calendar Feb-04-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
PES-22-305690, Estate of Lillian Wong, and
PTR-22-305818, California Revocable Living Trust of Lillian Wong

Appearances:
Jason J. Granskog and Pamela Ty for Joyce L. Wong, Petitioner.
Jeffrey Loew and Ciaran O'Sullivan for Ray Torres, Respondent.

The court acknowledged the status reports filed by both sides. Discussion was held regarding the evidentiary hearing scheduled for February 28, 2025. Mr. O'Sullivan requested the hearing be continued to August to consolidate multiple overlapping issues, including ownership of a $2 million home, an estate bank account dispute ($30,000), a pension fund ($70,000), and a vehicle. The court denied the request to continue the evidentiary hearing.

Mr. Granskog argued that Ray Torres improperly withdrew $37,000 from the estate after the decedent's death and must return it. Mr. O'Sullivan contended that the funds were used for funeral and estate-related expenses, potentially exempting him from liability under the probate code. The court acknowledged that Mr. Torres has now admitted to taking the money, shifting the legal question to whether he was entitled to do so without a formal creditor's claim.

The court informed the parties that it will issue a case management order requiring each side to submit a list of witnesses, brief summary of testimony, list of exhibits to be used at the hearing, etc. A briefing schedule on whether Mr. Torres can be charged rent while ownership is disputed and legal arguments regarding the return of estate funds will also be issued.

The court directed the courtroom clerk to place two petitions in the trust matter [(1) Petition to Confirm Real Property Listed on Schedule Attached to Trust as Trust Asset, filed by Ray Torres on 11/13/2022, and (2) Petition to Invalidate Trust and Confirm Real and Personal Property Belongs to the Estate of Lillian Wong, filed by Nelson Wong on 3/17/2023] which were formerly set for trial in Dept 206, back on the probate calendar for February 28, 2025, to be addressed at the evidentiary hearing.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Petition Pursuant To Probate Code 850
Off Calendar Oct-22-2024 Continued To Feb-04-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-22-305690 Estate of Lillian Wong and
PTR-22-305818 The Lillian Wong Trust.

Appearances: Pamela Ty and Jason Granskog appeared by zoom for Joyce Wong (administrator).
Jeffrey Loew appeared by zoom and Ciarin O'Sullivan appeared in person for Ray Torres.

The parties reported that they have agreed to do global mediation and to postpone Joyce's deposition until after mediation is completed. Further discussion was held regarding the urgency to determine who shall pay the property insurance as the policy expires on November 13th, setting of an evidentiary hearing (Dept. 204) and trial setting (Dept. 206), the outstanding procedural notice issues, there being no need for a Breslin order, etc. Mrs. Ty stated that they have withdrawn their claims with respect to the teamster's pension and the 401K and stated that the issue of the Costco visa is already included in the withdrawals that were made by Mr. Torres. The Petition for Order Requiring Return of Estate Property, etc. and the status hearing on petition pursuant to Probate Code 850 are continued to February 4, 2025, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 (trial on the real property ownership issue will be set at that time). The evidentiary hearing on the issue of ownership of the bank accounts, the car and the rental valuation in the estate matter is set for February 28, 2025, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are ordered to meet and confer no later than October 29, 2024 on the issue of payment of the property insurance. If the parties are not able to come to an agreement, Mrs. Ty and Mr. Granskog are to file an ex parte petition for an order requiring that Mr. Torres to pay the insurance.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Colhour. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Petition Pursuant To Probate Code 850
Off Calendar Aug-21-2024 Continued To Oct-22-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-22-305690 Estate of Lillian Wong and
PTR-22-305818 The California Revocable Living Trust of Lillian Wong.

Appearances: Pamela Ty and Jason Granskog appeared by zoom for Joyce Wong (successor trustee/successor administrator).
Jeffrey Loew appeared by zoom and Ciaran O'Sullivan appeared in person for Ray Torres (respondent).

The court stated that it was disappointed that the status reports weren't joint status reports and that the parties should try harder to file them jointly if they are ordered to do so. Discussion was held regarding the only issue having to do with the money that was taken by Mr. Torres after Mrs. Wong was deceased, the creditor's claim being a separate issue and there being a civil case, the issue of whether the parties are following the proper procedures and whether they should file a motion for summary adjudication, there being no representative appointed in the Contra Costa County case, the estate assets being solely Lillian Wong's, the trust assets not being the same as the estate assets, the $23,000.00 outstanding property tax bill, the only issues before the court being the bank accounts, the car and the credit cards, etc. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that he does not waive defective notice of the 850 petition and that he is not willing to waive service of the amendment. Further discussion was held regarding the issue of Mr. Torres allowing service on his attorney rather than direct service. The Petition for Order Requiring Return of Estate Property, etc. and the status hearing on petition pursuant to Probate Code 850 are continued to October 22, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Magdaleno. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Petition Pursuant To Probate Code 850
Off Calendar Jul-10-2024 Continued To Aug-21-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-22-305690 Estate of Lillian Wong and
PTR-22-305818 The California Revocable Living Trust of Lillian Wong.

Appearances: Pamela Ty appeared by zoom for Joyce Wong (successor trustee).
Jason Granskog appeared by zoom for Joyce Wong (special administrator of the Estate of Nelson Wong).
Jeffrey Loew appeared by zoom for Ray Torres (petitioner/respondent).

The court stated that it saw the supplement and the objections that were filed. Discussion was held regarding mediation having been talked about last year, but the parties not knowing where they are now, the issue of the procedural facts becoming more complex, whether the trust matter can go off calendar, Mr. Torres having taken money from the estate, Mr. Torres having been a care giver, Mr. Loew's position that Mr. Torres was the decedent's partner, the request that the court order Mr. Torres to immediately return all of the assets to the estate, the trust and the estate not having any causes of action in common and there being no need to consolidate, Mr. Torres not having paid any rent even though he was asked to do so, etc. The Petition for Order Requiring Return of Estate Property Pursuant to Prob. Code 850, and status on a petition pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 in the trust matter are continued to August 21, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court informed counsel that it will issue an order requiring the parties to file a joint status report which will probably be due by August 14, 2024 and stated that it would also like some information as to why the trust matter can't be taken off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Petition Pursuant To Probate Code 850
Off Calendar May-01-2024 Continued To Jul-10-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jeffrey Loew appeared by zoom for Ray Torres (petitioner/respondent).
Jason Granskog appeared by zoom for Nelson Wong (petitioner/respondent) now deceased.

The court informed Mr. Granskog that his ex parte petition substituting in the wife will be granted. Mr. Granskog stated that he also filed an ex parte petition for the same in the estate matter. Status re petition pursuant to Probate Code Section 850 is continued to July 10, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be heard with the estate case.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Aug-08-2023 Continued To Sep-26-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard: PES-22-305690 Estate of Lillian Wong and PTR-22-305818 California revocable living trust if Lillian Wong.

Appearances: Jason Granskog and Pamela Ty appearing via zoom for Nelson Wong (petitioner).
James Lamping appearing via zoom for Ray Torres (petitioner).

Discussion was held regarding procedural defects on both petitions filed, that there is an urgency on appointing an administrator, and that many issues learned of recently due to the delivery of the documents being produce just recently. Petition for letters of administration and second amended petition for letters of administration are taken under submission. Status of mediation is continued to September 26, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Papers to be filed by September 12, 2023.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Cabrera. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas
Off Calendar Jun-08-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jason Granskog appeared by zoom for Nelson Wong (respondent/petitioner).
James Lamping appeared by telephone for Ray Torres.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions in response to the court's tentative ruling as well as the court's inquiries. Upon conclusion of argument, the court ruled as follows: 1) The subpoenaed medical records from CPMS Davies are limited to any document Decedent signed, and limited in time to one month before and one month after March 2021 [when the disputed Trust instrument was signed]. Mr. Torres ought to provide the names of Decedent's treating doctors, to the extent he has that information, and that should be done informally. 2) The subpoenaed records from Cypress Lawn funeral home are limited to documents having the Decedent's signature and the whole document of any Advance Health Care Directive of Decedent's. 3) The subpoenaed records from the Teamsters Local are limited to signature pages with Decedent's signature. 4) The subpoenaed records from Chase Bank are limited to documents after January 1, 2018 and to accounts in Decedent Lillian Wong's name only, including signature cards, applications for new accounts, credit card accounts, and canceled checks in her own accounts. Sanctions are denied. Mr. Lamping is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
